reasoning: As of October 26, 2025, Ariarne Titmus holds the long course world record for the 200m freestyle with a time of 1:52.23, set at the Australian Olympic Trials in June 2024. The 2025 major summer competitions, including the World Aquatics Championships in Singapore, have already passed without the record being broken. Long-course swimming world records are extremely rarely broken during the late autumn and winter months, as the swimming calendar transitions primarily to short course events (including the December Short Course World Championships) and athletes are typically deep into training phases rather than fully tapered for peak performances. Even if athletes like Mollie O'Callaghan or Summer McIntosh compete at typical December long course meets (e.g., Queensland Championships or US Open), reaching the world record time of 1:52.23 is highly improbable outside of peak championship tapers. Because World Aquatics has already ratified Titmus's record, it will only change if someone breaks it or if she is retroactively disqualified, both of which are extremely unlikely between October 2025 and January 2026. Therefore, the probability that she still holds the record on the resolution dates is very high, decreasing only slightly across the horizon to account for unforeseen off-season performances or administrative outcomes.
